info.name: Kang Open Banking API v4.53.1
info.description:
Auto-generated from OpenAPI 4.51.5.

Base URLs:
  - Sandbox:    https://sandbox-api.kangopenbanking.com/v1
  - Production: https://api.kangopenbanking.com/v1

Set `base_url` and `access_token` (or `api_key`) in the environment.

header.description: Client-supplied correlation id (UUID v4 recommended). Echoed back in the `X-Request-ID` response header and persisted across edge functions, ledger writes and outbound webhook deliveries for distributed tracing.

W3C Trace Context Level 2 traceparent header. When supplied, KOB propagates the same context to every upstream call (bank connectors, mobile-money providers, settlement workers) and emits the resulting span IDs to OTLP collectors. See https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/
W3C Trace Context Level 2 tracestate header. Vendor-specific trace data, propagated unchanged.

BCP 47 language tag(s) for response localisation. Supported: `en`, `en-CM`, `en-GB`, `fr`, `fr-CM`. Defaults to `en` when omitted. RFC 7807 `ProblemDetails.detail` and `title` are translated when a French tag is supplied.

header.description: Client-supplied correlation id (UUID v4 recommended). Echoed back in the `X-Request-ID` response header and persisted across edge functions, ledger writes and outbound webhook deliveries for distributed tracing.

W3C Trace Context Level 2 traceparent header. When supplied, KOB propagates the same context to every upstream call (bank connectors, mobile-money providers, settlement workers) and emits the resulting span IDs to OTLP collectors. See https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/
W3C Trace Context Level 2 tracestate header. Vendor-specific trace data, propagated unchanged.

BCP 47 language tag(s) for response localisation. Supported: `en`, `en-CM`, `en-GB`, `fr`, `fr-CM`. Defaults to `en` when omitted. RFC 7807 `ProblemDetails.detail` and `title` are translated when a French tag is supplied.

query.description: PKCE code challenge. The Base64URL-encoded SHA-256 hash of the code_verifier (S256 method). REQUIRED by FAPI 1.0 Advanced Section 5.2.2 for all authorization requests. The corresponding code_verifier must be provided at the token endpoint.
PKCE code challenge method. MUST be S256 (SHA-256). Plain method is NOT supported per FAPI 1.0 Advanced security requirements.

Cryptographically random nonce value associating the client session with the ID Token and preventing replay attacks. REQUIRED by FAPI 1.0 Advanced Section 5.2.2-14 for all authorization requests. Must be unique per request with at least 128 bits of entropy (minimum 22 Base64url characters). The server MUST include the nonce claim in the issued ID Token.
FAPI 1.0 Advanced: URI referencing a Pushed Authorization Request (PAR). Mutually exclusive with request.
FAPI 1.0 Advanced: Signed JWT containing the authorization request parameters (Request Object). Mutually exclusive with request_uri.
Space-separated string of requested Authentication Context Class Reference values. e.g. "urn:openbanking:psd2:sca"
JSON object requesting specific claims in the ID Token or UserInfo response (OIDC Core Section 5.5).

W3C Trace Context Level 2 traceparent header. When supplied, KOB propagates the same context to every upstream call (bank connectors, mobile-money providers, settlement workers) and emits the resulting span IDs to OTLP collectors. See https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/
W3C Trace Context Level 2 tracestate header. Vendor-specific trace data, propagated unchanged.

BCP 47 language tag(s) for response localisation. Supported: `en`, `en-CM`, `en-GB`, `fr`, `fr-CM`. Defaults to `en` when omitted. RFC 7807 `ProblemDetails.detail` and `title` are translated when a French tag is supplied.

Required on all POST/PUT endpoints. Unique key for idempotent request processing (UUID v4 recommended). Keys expire after 24 hours.

Client-supplied correlation id (UUID v4 recommended). Echoed back in the `X-Request-ID` response header and persisted across edge functions, ledger writes and outbound webhook deliveries for distributed tracing.

W3C Trace Context Level 2 traceparent header. When supplied, KOB propagates the same context to every upstream call (bank connectors, mobile-money providers, settlement workers) and emits the resulting span IDs to OTLP collectors. See https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/
